title: How HealthCore AI Learns Your Body's Rhythm — And Alerts You Before Something's Wrong
excerpt: A deep dive into the on-device AI engine that powers every Lumivex. No cloud. No latency. No privacy tradeoffs.
author: Dr. Marcus Chen
content:
Most health AI runs in the cloud. Your data leaves your device, travels to a server, gets processed, and sends a result back. That round trip takes 200-800 milliseconds. It also means someone else's computer has your heart rate data.

We decided that was unacceptable. So we built an AI engine that runs entirely on your wrist.

The 72-Hour Learning Window

When you first put on a Lumivex, HealthCore AI enters a 72-hour learning phase. It's not idle — it's building your personal baseline. Your resting heart rate range. Your HRV patterns. Your sleep architecture. Your activity signature. Your skin temperature rhythm.

This is fundamentally different from how most health wearables work. They compare you to population averages. HealthCore compares you to you — specifically, to your last 72 hours of data.

Why 72 hours? Because that's the minimum window to capture two full sleep cycles plus a workday and a rest day. Any shorter and you miss patterns. Any longer and the model becomes sluggish to real changes.

Anomaly Detection, Not Diagnosis

Let's be precise about what HealthCore does and doesn't do. It flags anomalies — deviations from your personal baseline that cross statistical thresholds. It does not diagnose medical conditions. That distinction matters.

When your resting heart rate spikes 15 BPM above baseline at 3 AM, HealthCore alerts you. That spike could be a fever, dehydration, stress, or the start of something serious. The AI doesn't guess — it surfaces the pattern and lets you (and your doctor) interpret it.

In our clinical validation with 1,200 participants, HealthCore's AFib detection algorithm achieved 98.2% sensitivity and 97.8% specificity compared to a 12-lead clinical ECG. That's not a replacement for a cardiologist. But it's a very good early warning system.

All On-Device. Always.

The AI model is about 40MB — small enough to run on a watch chipset. It processes 200 sensor samples per second using a quantized neural network optimized for ARM Cortex-M processors. Raw biometric data never leaves your device unless you explicitly export it.

Your health data is yours. We built the entire system around that principle.
metaTitle: How HealthCore AI Works — On-Device Health Intelligence | Lumivex
metaDescription: HealthCore AI runs entirely on your wrist — no cloud, no latency, no privacy tradeoffs. Here's how the 72-hour learning window and anomaly detection work.

faq.question: Does HealthCore AI require a subscription?
faq.answer: Apex models include 1 year free, Pulse includes 6 months free. After the trial, it's $9.99/month or $89.99/year. Core and Pulse Kids include basic health tracking without the AI coaching features.

faq.question: Can I export my data for my doctor?
faq.answer: Yes. The Lumivex app can generate a clinical-format PDF report with any date range and metrics you choose. Share via email, AirDrop, or print.
